raw_step.input: python3 inline calculation for priority, classification, sensitivity, and budget checks
raw_step.observation: Computed priority scores, break scores, sensitivity classifications, and remediation combination costs using an inline Python snippet. Results: R1 priority 0.327333 and score 49.10; R2 priority 0.496667 and score 74.50; R3 priority 0.622000 and score 55.80; R4 priority 0.565667 and score 79.85. The -5% threshold shift flips R1 to break. Eligible remediation combos: O1+O2=128700, O1+O3=140250, O2+O3=166650, O1+O2+O3=217800.
raw_step.output: Established ranking R3 > R4 > R2 > R1; established expert_review trigger because one record changes class under the sensitivity shift; established feasible remediation combinations under budget.

task: Design reproducible, auditable detection rules (api_break_rules.yaml) and a concise release_note.md to support a program manager decision (hold, release, retest, redesign, or targeted mitigation) using only the embedded packet. Compute priority using the provided formula, evaluate remediation option combinations subject to the embedded remediation_budget_usd, run a 5% sensitivity check and flag expert_review if any record changes classification, document all assumptions, provide row-level citations to the embedded packet for all intermediate calculations, include validation checks and sensitivity notes, and produce a final decision table. Do not invent or source external data; mark unresolved ambiguities as needs_review.
